disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Long history of thick scaly patches on the extensor surfaces (elbows and knees) previously labelled as plaque psoriasis; the prescribed topical treatment has been neglected for weeks
trigger_question_examples: Have you had these patches before?
Do you have any skin conditions?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I've had these scaly patches on my elbows and knees for years — I was told it was plaque psoriasis. I have a cream for it but I stopped using it a few weeks ago.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The thick plaques fissured deeply on bending the elbow tonight, producing sharp pain and minor bleeding
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: When I bent my arms tonight the thick patches just split open — deep cracks that really sting and bled a bit.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient vigorously rubbed the area, after which the surrounding skin became red
trigger_question_examples: Did you do anything to the area?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I was rubbing and scratching at it a fair bit because it was sore, and after that the skin around it went red.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The redness is a narrow rim right around the plaques and, on the patient's own account, lightens when pressed
trigger_question_examples: Does the redness fade or go pale when you press on it?
How wide is the red area around the cracks?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's only a thin band right around the patches. When I press it, it does seem to go pale for a moment.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'm not really sure how to check that properly — it's hard to tell.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, rigors or chills reported
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fever, shivering or chills?
Do you feel unwell in yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever, no shakes or chills — I feel okay in myself apart from the sore arms.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No purulent, yellow or green discharge and no weeping exudate from the fissures
trigger_question_examples: Is there any pus, or yellow/green discharge?
Are the cracks weeping or crusting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, there's no pus or coloured discharge and nothing weeping — just a bit of blood from the cracks.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The redness is not tracking or spreading up the limb on the patient's account
trigger_question_examples: Is the redness spreading up your arm?
Are there any red streaks running away from the patches?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's staying right around the patches — I can't see it going up my arm or any streaks.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient can bend and straighten the elbow; the pain is at the skin surface, not deep inside the joint
trigger_question_examples: Can you fully bend and straighten your elbow?
Is the pain in the skin, or deep inside the joint?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can move the elbow okay — it's the skin that hurts when it stretches, not deep in the joint.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Environmental dryness and cold weather; topical psoriasis treatment neglected in the lead-up
trigger_question_examples: Has anything changed in your environment or routine?
Have you been using your usual creams?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's been really dry and cold at home lately, and I'll admit I stopped putting the creams on for the last few weeks.
